summary: Schema 0.2 reconciliation remains within workspace-infra: execution-state resolves the selected Story artifact route without a CLI reverse dependency, and targeted regression coverage passes.
inspection_summary: Inspected the HEAD delta, target model, Schema 0.2 architecture and spec, execution-state route resolution, guarded run-session dependency direction, CLI composition, fresh conformance, and targeted execution-state plus one-command runtime tests. regression_guard is covered by selected-route, legacy-route, and rollback cases; path_surface_coverage covers managed/source reconciliation for configured flat and Schema 0.2 feature_slug PR routes.
inspection_evidence: .vibepro/qa/conformance-a9109350-fresh.json; .vibepro/checks/architecture/2026-07-24T125154Z/check.md; command `node --test test/execution-state.test.js test/e2e/story-vibepro-one-command-pr-ready-closure-runtime.spec.ts` (56 passed, 0 failed); current-worktree codebase-memory-mcp index status, search_graph, and trace_path results.
inspection_inputs: codebase-memory-mcp current-worktree index status, search_graph, and trace_path
judgment_delta: Initial concern: Schema 0.2 reconciliation might couple execution state or run-session back into the CLI, or fabricate an ownership-probe Story. Final: pass because execution-state calls the shared artifact-routing resolver for the selected catalog Story, while run-session remains a CLI dependency target rather than a CLI caller; selected-route and legacy rollback coverage passes.

Conformance initially required classification because its overall status is needs_review. Final: no new finding for this change; its 22 review-level responsibility hotspots are existing static-scan results, including CLI, and do not include changed execution-state. API-boundary, authorization-order, and duplicate-query checks pass with zero blocks.
